title: Helsinki Event Aggregator
tagline: A high-speed tool for gathering city-wide event data.
problem: Helsinki's event information is scattered across multiple websites. Checking these sources one by one caused the app to take over a minute to load.
solution: I built a system in Python that fetches data from all sources simultaneously.
architecture: The project uses a Producer-Consumer model on AWS. One Lambda function handles data ingestion and automatic TTL cleanup, while a second function serves the filtered API. GitHub Actions manages the automated deployment process.

title: Secure File Submission API
tagline: A safe gateway for file uploads with integrated malware detection.
problem: Public upload forms are a major security risk, as they can be used to send malicious files or viruses directly to a server.
solution: I created a backend that automatically scans every incoming file for threats. Files are only delivered to the administrator if they are verified as safe.
architecture: Using FastAPI and the VirusTotal API, the system checks file types and safety levels in the background. The entire application is packaged in a Docker container for consistent setup and security.

title: IMDb CLI Manager
tagline: A command-line tool for organizing personal movie and review databases.
problem: Managing a growing movie collection while keeping reviews and film details synchronized in a cloud-based NoSQL database can be difficult.
solution: This Python tool connects directly to a cloud database to handle entries, calculate average user ratings, and ensure data remains consistent when items are deleted.
architecture: The application uses PyMongo to communicate with MongoDB Atlas. I designed logic to maintain database health, ensuring that when a movie is removed, its associated reviews are also cleared.

title: Dynamic Social Network
tagline: A responsive social platform for real-time posts and interactions.
problem: Many web applications feel sluggish because the entire page reloads every time a user interacts with a post.
solution: I used JavaScript to process likes and comments in the background. This allows the interface to update instantly, providing a much smoother experience for the user.
architecture: This project uses Django for the core logic and JavaScript for a modern, reload-free interface. The layout is built with Bootstrap to ensure it works well on both mobile devices and desktop screens.
